title: Onuris hauthalii
intro: Onuris hauthalii, also known as Astragalus hauthalii, is a plant species within the pea family (Fabaceae) that is native to China. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to boost the immune system, reduce stress, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Astragalus hauthalii include saponins, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Astragalus hauthalii is commonly prepared as a decoction or dried extract, often combined with other herbs, to create tonifying and adaptogenic preparations for the purpose of enhancing overall well-being.
taxonomy: Onuris hauthalii is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a rare, endangered succulent native to Namibia. The species was first described by botanist Moritz Kurt Dinter in 1931. Onuris hauthalii is considered an endemism in the Namib Desert, and its survival is threatened by habitat destruction. As a result, the plant has been listed as Critically Endangered on the IUCN Red List.
